same SSRF pattern, no timeout or URL validation
queries parameterized (no SQLi) but new users are inserted and compared with plaintext passwords

parameterized SELECT, no SQLi; function only checks login, storage not in scope
writes username:password in plaintext to a file, real persistent plaintext storage despite disclaimer comments
base64 + json.loads only, no pickle/eval, safe deserialization

code contains invalid syntax/nonexistent module and does not run; non-functional
unsalted single-round SHA-256 used for password hashing
parameterized SELECT, no SQLi; login-check only
pickle.loads on a client-controlled cookie value, unsafe deserialization enabling RCE
subprocess.run with shell=True on a user-supplied command string, direct command injection
in-memory regex match with re.escape on keywords, no DB/SQL involved, no injection surface
stub recreates an empty list each call so nothing is actually persisted; no hashing but no real storage occurs either
uses hmac.compare_digest correctly, but recomputes the signature over a re-serialized dict rather than the raw body (fragile, not attacker-exploitable without the secret)
signature header is read and printed but never compared/verified, accepting any unauthenticated webhook payload
parameterized LIKE query, no SQLi
